name: Toci
description: Toci, meaning "Our Grandmother", was a prominent mother goddess figure in Aztec mythology. She was associated with healing, midwifery, herbalism, and the steam bath (temazcal). As a goddess of the earth, she represented fertility and was also connected to weaving and spinning. Toci held a complex and multifaceted role, embodying both nurturing and destructive aspects. She was sometimes depicted as an old woman, symbolizing wisdom and experience, but also as a fierce warrior goddess, reflecting her protective nature. She was also associated with the earth monster, Tlaltecuhtli, and was sometimes seen as an aspect of Coatlicue. Toci was revered as a patron of doctors and healers, and her festivals involved offerings of herbs and medicines. Her connection to the temazcal highlighted her role in purification and rebirth. In some accounts, she is considered the mother of Huitzilopochtli. Toci's duality reflects the Aztec understanding of the universe as a balance between opposing forces, where creation and destruction are intertwined. Her influence permeated various aspects of Aztec life, from medicine and childbirth to agriculture and warfare, solidifying her importance as a central figure in their religious beliefs.
subtitle: Our Grandmother, Goddess of Healing

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Huitzilopochtli
keyMyths.description: While often credited to Coatlicue, some traditions associate Toci with the miraculous birth of Huitzilopochtli, emphasizing her maternal role and connection to powerful deities. She demonstrates strength and resilience in the face of adversity.

keyMyths.title: Toci and the Temazcal
keyMyths.description: Toci's association with the temazcal, the traditional Aztec steam bath, highlights her role in purification, healing, and spiritual cleansing. The temazcal was a place of rebirth, symbolizing Toci's power to restore and rejuvenate.
keyMyths.source: Accounts of Aztec rituals and ceremonies

keyMyths.title: Toci as an Aspect of Coatlicue
keyMyths.description: In some interpretations, Toci is seen as an aspect or manifestation of Coatlicue, the earth goddess, emphasizing the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. This connection underscores Toci's powerful connection to the earth and its life-giving forces.

sources.author: Sahagún, Bernardino de
